mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals weakening labor market and lower economic growth expectations, which drives demand for safe-haven assets and compresses long-term yields.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is rising month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) signals deflation risk, leading the market to anticipate further monetary accommodation and pushing long-term yields lower.
failure_condition: If the 60-day yield return is positive in more than one-third of episodes triggered by the deflation threshold over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous sharp declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate a severe demand collapse, reinforcing recession fears and driving yields down as growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline more than 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 40% of instances where both series meet the joint decline threshold over any 5-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a recession (UNRATE above 6%), a decline in industrial production (INDPRO) amplifies supply-side weakness and pushes yields lower as the market prices in prolonged economic slack.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield return is positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO falls and UNRATE is above 6% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and unemployment is rising, the combined signal of aggressive easing and labor market deterioration intensifies safe-haven demand, causing yields to fall more sharply than either factor alone.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change is negative less than 60% of the time when both conditions hold over any 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a broad economic contraction, prompting investors to move into safe assets and reduce yield expectations, leading to lower long-term yields.
failure_condition: If the 60-day yield change is positive (yield rises) in more than half of quarters following a GDP decline over the past 20 years.

mechanism_narrative: A massive monthly drop in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS > 500k decline) signals acute labor market distress, triggering a flight to safety and a sharp decline in yields over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: If the 5-day yield return is positive (yield rises) in more than 25% of episodes with a payroll decline exceeding 500k over any 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-bound interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals that the Fed's accommodative stance may eventually lead to overheating, pushing long-term yields higher as term premiums reprice.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield return is negative when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% in more than half of occurrences over a 3-year walk-forward period.
